mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and persistent inflation compresses real corporate profit margins while forcing prolonged monetary tightening, jointly depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when both GDP growth decelerates and CPIAUCSL exceeds 255, contradicting the joint negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding historical capacity thresholds indicates robust manufacturing utilization and healthy supply-side momentum, supporting near-term earnings revisions and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in over 50% of instances where INDPRO sustains values above 110.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding consumer revolving credit balances reflect increasing household liquidity and consumption capacity, driving upward revisions in retail earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where RSAFS shows sequential growth, falsifying the liquidity-to-equity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with elevated price indices signal entrenched inflation expectations, forcing sustained financial conditions tightening and negative medium-term equity returns as discount rates remain structurally elevated.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day forward returns when both FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0% and CPIAUCSL remains above 250, breaking the joint tightening signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong nonfarm payroll growth and sub-4% unemployment indicate a resilient labor market that supports corporate revenue growth without triggering aggressive monetary policy responses, boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k and UNRATE stays below 4.0%, invalidating the dual labor market support hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.5 million annualized units signal strong construction demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, providing an immediate positive catalyst for equity markets through wealth and investment channels.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of months where HOUST prints above 1500, refuting the housing-confidence equity transmission mechanism.
